verdict_reasoning: ITPR1 is the canonical IP3 receptor — a multi-pass ER membrane channel mediating Ca2+ release from the ER lumen into cytosol. Its TM domains and large regulatory/ligand-binding domain reside on the ER membrane, not the PM. Walking the contextual buckets: (a) cell_state_induced — no documented ecto-display in cancer/cell-death/activation; (b) tissue_restricted_surface — no germline/developmental outer-leaflet pool; (c) lysosomal_exocytosis — not a lysosomal protein; (d) dual_localization — no documented PM minority pool of the channel itself (some reports place IP3R at ER-PM junctions, but luminal face stays luminal, not extracellular); (e) stable_surface_attachment — not secreted, no TM-partner tethering. Endomembrane-resident ER channel.
